name: Ascent to the Castillo de Segura de la Sierra — circular by Orcera
name_original: Subida al Castillo de Segura de la Sierra — circular por Orcera
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Segura De La Sierra traverse a landscape characterized by vast mountain ranges, extensive forests, and winding roads. The region features significant elevation changes, with routes often passing through infinite olive groves and dramatic limestone peaks. This area, part of Spain's largest protected natural park, offers diverse terrain for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Segura De La Sierra The most popular road cycling route is Ascent to the Castillo de Segura de la Sierra — circular by Orcera, a 23.3 miles (37.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 59 minutes to complete. This moderate route includes an ascent to the historic castle, offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is C.F. El Campillo Shelter – Segura de la Sierra loop from Cementerio Municipal, a difficult 67.1 miles (108.0 km) path. This challenging loop features significant elevation gain through varied mountain scenery. Local road cyclists also love the Puerto del Bellotar – Bienservida loop from Segura de la Sierra, a 59.4 miles (95.7 km) trail leading through mountain passes and winding roads, often completed in about 5 hours 21 minutes. Road cycling around Segura De La Sierra is defined by infinite olive groves, winding mountain roads, and dramatic limestone peaks. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Segura De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Segura De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Segura De La Sierra?
A: There are over 15 dedicated road cycling routes around Segura De La Sierra. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 7 classified as moderate and 11 as difficult, offering significant elevation changes and diverse terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Segura De La Sierra?
A: The region offers a varied and engaging terrain for road cyclists. You can expect mostly paved surfaces, winding roads through infinite olive groves, hilly landscapes, and dramatic limestone peaks. Routes often feature significant elevation changes, providing both a physical challenge and breathtaking scenery.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate road cycling routes suitable for less experienced riders?
A: Yes, while many routes are challenging, there are moderate options available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1277897445/ascent-to-the-castillo-de-segura-de-la-sierra-circular-by-orcera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Castillo de Segura de la Sierra — circular by Orcera&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 37.4 km trail that includes an ascent to the historic castle, offering panoramic views.

Q: What are some of the more challenging road cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced cyclists, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40302884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;C.F. El Campillo Shelter – Segura de la Sierra loop from Cementerio Municipal&lt;/a&gt; offer a difficult 108 km path with significant elevation gain through varied mountain scenery. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24524263" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Bellotar – Bienservida loop from Segura de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, a 95.7 km trail leading through mountain passes and winding roads.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while road cycling in Segura De La Sierra?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle past features like the source of the Segura River, explore areas with alpine canyons, and enjoy panoramic views from various points. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2305421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Despiernacaballos Pass&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural monument you might encounter on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31859005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water source – Despiernacaballos Pass loop from Huelga Utrera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or water features accessible by road bike?
A: While some waterfalls might require a short hike, you can find routes that pass near significant water features. The region boasts endless rivers and streams with clean, turquoise or emerald waters. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5391926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Toba Spring&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3168204" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Aceite&lt;/a&gt; are examples of beautiful water highlights in the area.

Q: What historical landmarks or viewpoints can I visit along the cycling routes?
A: Segura de la Sierra itself is a Historic-Artistic Site. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1277897445/ascent-to-the-castillo-de-segura-de-la-sierra-circular-by-orcera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Castillo de Segura de la Sierra — circular by Orcera&lt;/a&gt;, lead to the Mudejar Castle, offering impressive panoramic views of the entire Sierra del Segura. The village also features historic buildings like the Santa María del Collado Parish Church and an ancient quadrangular bullring.

Q: When is the best time of year to go road cycling in Segura De La Sierra?
A: The region offers diverse natural beauty throughout the seasons. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer. However, the winding mountain roads and extensive forests can be enjoyed in various conditions, depending on your preference for challenge and scenery.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Segura De La Sierra?
A: The road cycling routes in Segura De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the challenging climbs, breathtaking views, and the unique experience of cycling through infinite olive groves and dramatic mountain landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1277897445/ascent-to-the-castillo-de-segura-de-la-sierra-circular-by-orcera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Castillo de Segura de la Sierra — circular by Orcera&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornos de Segura – Hornos Viewpoint loop from Cementerio Municipal&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 66.5 km loop.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Segura De La Sierra?
A: While specific parking details vary by route, many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40302884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;C.F. El Campillo Shelter – Segura de la Sierra loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornos de Segura – Hornos Viewpoint loop&lt;/a&gt;, are described as starting from the Cementerio Municipal, which often indicates available parking facilities nearby. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for recommended starting points and parking information.
tips: Strategically located on the road that connects the Alto Guadalquivir region with the lands of La Mancha. The castle of Segura de la Sierra is one of the main cultural tourist attractions in the area. The Castle dates from the time of the Knights of the Order of Santiago and was built during the 13th and 14th centuries. Spectacular views of the entire landscape.
Small town located in the center of the Sierra de Segura on a hillside near the Amurjo river. Spectacular cycling scenery through a labyrinth of valleys, gorges and mountains.

waypoints.name: Castle of Segura de la Sierra
